## Candidate Plan — `swapi-grpc` (gRPC port of swapi.info)

> Revision 4 — addresses all blockers and warnings from revisions 1-3; the SOURCE path nitpick is addressed via D26 + the S1/A9 wording updates below.

### Objective

Create a fresh, opinionated Go project at `/Users/ycm/Developer/oss/swapi` that re-exposes the full swapi.info dataset (people, films, planets, species, starships, vehicles) as a **gRPC + Connect-protocol** service built on `connect-go` and `buf`. The server ships as a single self-contained binary; the data is embedded into the binary via `//go:embed`; the project compiles, passes `buf lint`, ships with a health check + reflection + CORS + graceful shutdown, and can be smoke-tested with `grpcurl`, `curl`, and a runnable example client.

### Constraints

- **Language/toolchain:** Go ≥ 1.22 (with `go 1.22` directive in `go.mod`), Buf v2 CLI, `protoc-gen-go`, `protoc-gen-connect-go` (v1.18+), `connectrpc/grpcreflect-go`, `connectrpc/connect-grpchealth-go`.
- **Transport:** gRPC over HTTP/2 and the Connect protocol over HTTP/1.1 from a single `net/http.Server`.
- **Data:** Embedded into the binary via `//go:embed` from a directory adjacent to the embedding Go file. No runtime network dependency.
- **API style:** Resource-oriented (one service per resource), unary RPCs only, no streaming.
- **Style:** snake_case in proto; stable field tags; proto package `swapi.v1`; `go_package` derived from a single `MODULE` Makefile variable, applied via `buf.gen.yaml`'s `go_package_prefix` (no sed).
- **License:**
  - Go code: **MIT** (`LICENSE`).
  - Embedded swapi data: derived from Wookieepedia / swapi; shipped under **CC-BY-SA 4.0** with a `NOTICE` file crediting Wookieepedia (https://starwars.fandom.com) and swapi (https://swapi.info).
  - README has an "Attribution & Data License" section.

### Non-goals

- Authentication, rate limiting, or any access control.
- Caching layer (data is small, embedded).
- Streaming RPCs, bidirectional flows, or server-push.
- Persisting edits — the dataset is read-only.
- A web UI, GraphQL gateway, or REST shim (use the Connect protocol directly).
- Auto-generating TS/Python/other-language clients (a Go example client is the demo).
- Cross-resource join RPCs.

### Module path

- **Default:** `github.com/example/swapi-grpc` (placeholder).
- **Single source of truth:** the Makefile exposes `MODULE ?= github.com/example/swapi-grpc`. `go.mod` and `buf.gen.yaml` (the literal `module:` line) both reference this string. The Makefile targets also reference the `MODULE` variable for `go mod edit -module` and similar; the YAML file holds a literal because Make variables do not expand inside YAML.
- **Override:** `make rename-module NEW=github.com/<you>/swapi-grpc` rewrites `go.mod`, the literal in `buf.gen.yaml`, and the Makefile variable in one shot. No proto file edits required.

### Data location

- The `//go:embed` directive cannot reach `data/*.json` at the repo root when the embedding Go file is in `internal/swapi/data/`.
- **Decision:** the canonical data location is `internal/swapi/data/data/`, adjacent to the embedding Go file. `make data` writes to `internal/swapi/data/data/` directly.
- `internal/swapi/data/data/SOURCE` records the upstream commit SHA used by the last `make data` run (informational; see A9). The SOURCE file lives alongside the JSON files in `internal/swapi/data/data/` so it ships with the embed.

### Assumptions

| # | Assumption | Resolution path |
|---|---|---|
| A1 | swapi.info data shape matches swapi.dev (field names, types, FK shape). | First step of S1 fetches all six lists and runs a programmatic schema sanity check (required fields present, expected counts ≥ 1, no duplicate IDs). If the check fails, `make data` exits non-zero and the plan revisits the `.proto` design before S2. |
| A2 | List responses are bare arrays; pagination is not used. | Confirmed by user choice. The proto still wraps `repeated X` in a `ListXResponse` message (gRPC requires this); no `next` / `previous` / `page` fields. |
| A3 | Resource IDs are preserved as **strings** (numeric `1` or slug `luke_skywalker` both fit). | Proto field is `string id`; loader does not coerce. **The example "luke_skywalker" is illustrative** — if swapi.info is confirmed to use numeric IDs at S1, the proto and tests still work because `string` is a strict superset. |
| A4 | FK fields are represented as `repeated string` of IDs (not full URLs). **Singular URL fields stay singular as `string`; repeated URL fields become `repeated string` of IDs.** The data loader strips the swapi base URL from each FK field and takes the trailing path segment as the ID. | Enforced centrally by `extractID(rawURL string) string` and `extractIDs(rawURLs []string) []string` helpers in `internal/swapi/data/data.go`. |
| A5 | Search is case-insensitive substring on `name` (or `title` for films). Empty `query` returns the same payload as `List` (D12). | Implemented server-side. Documented in the search RPC godoc and asserted by a unit test. |
| A6 | The `?schema` endpoint does not exist in swapi.info. | Not modeled. |
| A7 | Numeric-but-string fields (`height`, `mass`, `population`, `cost_in_credits`, etc.) stay as proto `string` because they can be `"unknown"` / `"n/a"`. | Documented in the proto file as a comment. |
| A8 | Timestamps (`created`, `edited`) stay as proto `string` (ISO-8601). | Documented. |
| A9 | Data snapshot is pinned to a specific upstream commit of `Juriy/swapi` (recorded in `internal/swapi/data/data/SOURCE`, alongside the JSON files). **Reproducibility is informational, not enforced** — the sanity test asserts shape (row counts, required fields, unique IDs) and fails `make data` if violated; the SHA in `SOURCE` is the audit trail. | `make data` always writes `SOURCE`; the shape check is what the test enforces. |
| A10 | The `internal/swapi/data/data/*.json` files are checked in; fetching them is a manual `make data` step, not part of the normal build. | Documented in the Makefile. |

### Resolved decisions

| # | Decision | Source |
|---|---|---|
| D1 | 6 services, 18 RPCs: `PeopleService` / `FilmsService` / `PlanetsService` / `SpeciesService` / `StarshipsService` / `VehiclesService`, each with `Get` + `List` + `Search`. | Grilling Q1 |
| D2 | Embedded data via `//go:embed data/*.json` from `internal/swapi/data/data.go` (data sits in `internal/swapi/data/data/`). | Grilling Q2 + revision 3 fix |
| D3 | No pagination — `List` returns the full embedded list in one response. | Grilling Q3 |
| D4 | gRPC server reflection via `connectrpc/grpcreflect-go` (`reflectv1.NewReflector` + `reflectv1.NewReflectServiceHandler`). | Grilling + reviewer |
| D5 | Browser CORS via `connect.WithCORS()` with **wildcard** origin (documented as demo-only in README). | Grilling + reviewer |
| D6 | Example client at `cmd/client/main.go`, including a NotFound call that prints the `connect.Code` and one Get per other resource. | Grilling + reviewer |
| D7 | Unit tests for each handler — one test file per resource under `internal/swapi/service/`. | Grilling + reviewer |
| D8 | gRPC Health v1 service via `connectrpc/connect-grpchealth-go`. Always-serving. | Reviewer |
| D9 | Configurable listen address via `-addr` flag with `PORT` env fallback (default `:8080`). | Reviewer |
| D10 | Graceful shutdown on `SIGINT` / `SIGTERM` with a 5s grace via `srv.Shutdown(ctx)`. End-to-end verification exercises **both** signals. | Reviewer |
| D11 | Error code contract — every handler returns `connect.NewError` with one of: `connect.CodeNotFound` (unknown id), `connect.CodeInvalidArgument` (malformed id; e.g. id containing characters not valid in the source's id charset), `connect.CodeInternal` (loader failure). | Reviewer |
| D12 | Empty search query returns the same payload as `List` (no error). | Grilling follow-up |
| D13 | Module path is a single Makefile variable with a `rename-module` target for post-clone rebranding. | Reviewer |
| D14 | Data license = CC-BY-SA 4.0 with `NOTICE` + README attribution section. Code license = MIT. | Grilling follow-up |
| D15 | The data loader uses **intermediate structs** that mirror the JSON field names, then transforms them into the generated proto types via a single conversion pass. **Direct `json.Unmarshal` into generated proto types is not used** because of the field renames (`homeworld` → `homeworld_id`, `films` → `film_ids`, `MGLT` → `mglt`, etc.). | Revision 3 fix |
| D16 | The `//go:embed` source directory is `internal/swapi/data/data/`, adjacent to the embedding Go file. | Revision 3 fix |
| D17 | The data sanity test is wired as a Makefile prerequisite of `make generate`, so a stale or broken data snapshot halts the build before codegen. | Revision 3 fix |
| D18 | Proto `go_package` is not set per-file. `buf.gen.yaml` uses `go_package_prefix` with a literal `module: github.com/example/swapi-grpc` and `path: gen`. The protoc-gen-go plugin generates Go code at `gen/swapi/v1/<fileBaseName>/<fileBaseName>.pb.go` (one Go package per `.proto` file). The protoc-gen-connect-go plugin generates `gen/swapi/v1/<fileBaseName>v1connect/<fileBaseName>.connect.go` and exposes the handler/client interfaces in the `<fileBaseName>v1connect` Go package. **Per-resource (per-file) naming throughout.** | Revision 3 + revision 4 fix |
| D19 | The test matrix exercises **malformed-id** cases (D11) with `id = "###"` or similar to assert `connect.CodeInvalidArgument`. | Revision 3 fix |
| D20 | Integration test (`internal/swapi/service/integration_test.go`) is written alongside the per-resource tests in S5; it does **not** import `cmd/client` (a main package). It replicates the example-client's call list against an `httptest.NewServer` to keep the test in-package. | Revision 3 fix |
| D21 | Handlers in S5 read fields directly: `person.Name`, `film.Title`, `species.Name`, etc. **No `Name()` method calls** (these are struct fields, not methods). | Revision 3 fix |
| D22 | `go.mod` ships with an explicit `go 1.22` directive. | Revision 3 fix |
| D23 | `buf.yaml` has no `name:` field by default; the project is local-only and is not published to BSR. (If the user later wants BSR, they can add `name: buf.build/<org>/swapi-grpc` to `buf.yaml`.) | Revision 3 fix |
| D24 | The Makefile exposes a `test-data` target (separate from the full `test` target) so `make generate` can depend on `make test-data` and not on the full unit test suite. | Revision 4 fix |
| D25 | The rendered `buf.gen.yaml` block contains the literal module string `github.com/example/swapi-grpc`. Make variables do not expand inside YAML, so `make rename-module` rewrites the literal in the YAML in addition to `go.mod` and the Makefile variable. | Revision 4 fix |
| D26 | The `SOURCE` file lives at `internal/swapi/data/data/SOURCE` (alongside the JSON files), not at `internal/swapi/data/SOURCE`. This keeps the SHA audit trail inside the embed-bundled directory. | Revision 4 fix |

### Implementation slices

| # | Slice | Deliverable | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|
| S0 | Bootstrap repo | `go.mod` (with `go 1.22` directive and all required deps), `Makefile` (with `MODULE` var; targets `data` `generate` `test-data` `test` `build` `run` `lint` `help` `rename-module`; `make generate` depends on `make test-data` per D24), `.gitignore` (`/gen`, `/bin`), `LICENSE` (MIT), `NOTICE` (Wookieepedia + swapi attribution, CC-BY-SA 4.0 pointer), `README.md` skeleton, `buf.yaml` (no `name:`), `buf.gen.yaml` (with the corrected `go_package_prefix` override per D18/D25) | `go mod tidy` works; `buf --version` works; `make help` lists every target (including `test-data`); `MODULE` variable substitution works; `make rename-module NEW=foo` is a no-op on the default module |
| S1 | Fetch & check in data | `make data` target does: (a) `git ls-remote` to capture the upstream commit of `Juriy/swapi`, (b) `curl` the six `data/<resource>.json` files from `raw.githubusercontent.com/Juriy/swapi/<sha>/data/...` to `internal/swapi/data/data/`, (c) write the SHA to `internal/swapi/data/data/SOURCE` (per D26), (d) `go test ./internal/swapi/data/...` (the `test-data` target) asserts required fields, ≥1 row per resource, unique IDs, and a sample-row FK transform. | `internal/swapi/data/data/*.json` non-empty; `internal/swapi/data/data/SOURCE` contains the SHA; `go test ./internal/swapi/data/...` passes; the sanity test fails `make data` when the loader is pointed at empty/wrong files; **`make generate` (a dependency of S2) is blocked by a failing sanity test** |
| S2 | Write protos | `proto/swapi/v1/{people,films,planets,species,starships,vehicles}.proto` — six files, one per resource. Each declares a `*Service` with `Get*` / `List*` / `Search*`, the resource message, request/response messages. **No `option go_package` per file** — D18. | `buf lint` clean |
| S3 | Generate code | `make generate` → `buf generate` produces **per-resource** Go packages: `gen/swapi/v1/people/people.pb.go`, `gen/swapi/v1/films/films.pb.go`, …, `gen/swapi/v1/planets/planets.pb.go`, `gen/swapi/v1/species/species.pb.go`, `gen/swapi/v1/starships/starships.pb.go`, `gen/swapi/v1/vehicles/vehicles.pb.go`. The connect-go plugin produces `gen/swapi/v1/people/peoplev1connect/people.connect.go` (and analogously for the other five resources), exposing `peoplev1connect.PeopleServiceHandler`, `peoplev1connect.NewPeopleServiceHandler`, `peoplev1connect.NewPeopleServiceClient`, etc. | `go build ./gen/...` succeeds; re-running `buf generate` after `rm -rf gen` produces an identical tree |
| S4 | Data loader | `internal/swapi/data/data.go` — single file with `//go:embed data/*.json`. **Two-pass load (D15):** (1) `json.Unmarshal` into an intermediate struct `rawPerson`, `rawFilm`, etc. that mirrors the JSON field names (e.g. `Homeworld string`, `Films []string`, `MGLT string`); (2) a `convert()` method transforms each intermediate struct into the generated proto type, applying `extractID` to FK URLs and renaming `MGLT` → `mglt`, `homeworld` → `homeworld_id`, `films` → `film_ids`, etc. The two helpers `extractID` and `extractIDs` are also tested directly. | `go test ./internal/swapi/data/...` covers: row counts, intermediate→proto rename correctness, `extractID("https://swapi.info/api/planets/1")` → `"1"`, `extractIDs(...)` preserves order and drops empty entries, required-field presence, ID uniqueness, and a sample full-record transform for one row per resource. |
| S5 | Service handlers + handler tests | `internal/swapi/service/{people,films,planets,species,starships,vehicles}.go` plus matching `<resource>_test.go` **and** `integration_test.go`. Each handler imports its own generated connect package (per S3's per-resource layout): `peoplev1connect`, `filmsv1connect`, etc. Each implements the `<resource>v1connect.<Resource>ServiceHandler` interface, holds a `*data.Data` field, and dispatches: `Get` → linear scan with case-insensitive id match, returning `connect.NewError(connect.CodeNotFound, ...)` on miss, `connect.NewError(connect.CodeInvalidArgument, ...)` on a clearly malformed id (e.g. id containing characters invalid for the source schema, see D11); `List` → return the slice; `Search` → case-insensitive substring on `.Name` / `.Title` (D21) with empty query returning the full list (per D12). | `go build ./internal/...` succeeds; `go test ./internal/swapi/service/...` passes the per-resource test matrix (see "Test cases" below) including the malformed-id cases (D19); the integration test in `integration_test.go` spins up `httptest.NewServer`, mounts the six handlers, and calls one Get per resource (replicating the example-client's call list) |
| S6 | Server entrypoint | `cmd/server/main.go` wires: all 6 service handlers via the per-resource generated `<resource>v1connect.New<Resource>ServiceHandler` factories (e.g. `peoplev1connect.NewPeopleServiceHandler`); gRPC reflection via `connectrpc.com/grpcreflect`; gRPC Health v1 via `connectrpc.com/connect-grpchealth-go`; `connect.WithCompressMinBytes(1024)` + `connect.WithCompressGzip()`; wildcard CORS via `connect.WithCORS()`; `http.Server` bound to `*addr` (flag `-addr`, env `PORT`); graceful shutdown trapping `SIGINT`/`SIGTERM` with a 5s `srv.Shutdown(ctx)` grace; structured logging via `log/slog`. | `./bin/server` starts on `:8080`; `./bin/server -addr :9000` starts on `:9000`; `PORT=9001 ./bin/server` starts on `:9001`; `kill -TERM $PID` exits 0 within 5s; `kill -INT $PID` exits 0 within 5s |
| S7 | Example client | `cmd/client/main.go` calls: `GetPerson("1")`, `ListPeople`, `SearchPeople("luke")`, `SearchPeople("")` (empty-query behavior), `GetPerson("does-not-exist")` (prints `connect.CodeNotFound`), plus one Get per other resource (`GetFilm("1")`, `GetPlanet("1")`, `GetSpecies("1")`, `GetStarship("2")`, `GetVehicle("4")`). Uses the per-resource `<resource>v1connect.New<Resource>ServiceClient(http.DefaultClient, baseURL)` factories. `baseURL` is a `-url` flag (default `http://localhost:8080`). | `go run ./cmd/client -url http://localhost:8080` prints expected output (Luke, full list, search hits, empty-query result identical to list, and a `NotFound` line) |
| S8 | _(deprecated, merged into S5)_ | _Per-resource test expansion and integration coverage now live in S5. The slice is kept here to preserve ordering for downstream readers but is a no-op; CI is gated on `go test ./...` from S5 onwards._ | n/a |
| S9 | README & polish | README with project description, data attribution (`Wookieepedia` + `swapi.info` + `CC-BY-SA 4.0`), `-addr` flag and `PORT` env docs, build/test/run commands, gRPC + Connect + curl examples, `make rename-module` usage, `make data` snapshot workflow. | Manual code-block review; all examples in the README also appear in the end-to-end verification block below |

### Per-resource proto field table

The convention is now explicit: **singular URL FKs stay as `string`; repeated URL FKs become `repeated string` of IDs.** This matches the species row in revision 2 and the reference proto's `string homeworld_id = 10;` on Person.

| Resource | Service | Singular fields (string) | `repeated string` FK IDs | int32 fields | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| people | `PeopleService` | `id`, `name`, `height`, `mass`, `hair_color`, `skin_color`, `eye_color`, `birth_year`, `gender`, `homeworld_id`, `created`, `edited` | `film_ids`, `species_ids`, `vehicle_ids`, `starship_ids` | none | `homeworld` is a singular URL → `string homeworld_id` (one element, kept singular per A4) |
| films | `FilmsService` | `id`, `title`, `opening_crawl`, `director`, `producer`, `release_date`, `created`, `edited` | `character_ids`, `planet_ids`, `starship_ids`, `vehicle_ids`, `species_ids` | `episode_id` (1–6) | `episode_id` is the only true int |
| planets | `PlanetsService` | `id`, `name`, `rotation_period`, `orbital_period`, `diameter`, `climate`, `gravity`, `terrain`, `surface_water`, `population`, `created`, `edited` | `resident_ids`, `film_ids` | none | |
| species | `SpeciesService` | `id`, `name`, `classification`, `designation`, `average_height`, `skin_colors`, `hair_colors`, `eye_colors`, `average_lifespan`, `homeworld_id`, `language`, `created`, `edited` | `people_ids`, `film_ids` | none | `homeworld` → `string homeworld_id` (singular) |
| starships | `StarshipsService` | `id`, `name`, `model`, `manufacturer`, `cost_in_credits`, `length`, `max_atmosphering_speed`, `crew`, `passengers`, `cargo_capacity`, `consumables`, `hyperdrive_rating`, `mglt`, `starship_class`, `created`, `edited` | `pilot_ids`, `film_ids` | none | JSON `MGLT` → proto `mglt` (snake_case) |
| vehicles | `VehiclesService` | `id`, `name`, `model`, `manufacturer`, `cost_in_credits`, `length`, `max_atmosphering_speed`, `crew`, `passengers`, `cargo_capacity`, `consumables`, `vehicle_class`, `created`, `edited` | `pilot_ids`, `film_ids` | none | |

### Per-resource test case matrix (S5)

For each of the 6 resources, the matching `<resource>_test.go` covers:

| Test | Input | Expected |
|---|---|---|
| `TestGet_Existing` | `id = "<known id>"` (the first row's id) | 200, message matches the embedded record (field-by-field equality) |
| `TestGet_NotFound` | `id = "this-does-not-exist"` | `connect.CodeNotFound` error, no payload |
| `TestGet_MalformedId` | `id = "###"` (clearly not a valid id in the source schema) | `connect.CodeInvalidArgument` error (D11, D19) |
| `TestList` | empty request | 200, `len(.<resource>s) == count`, count matches `len(embedded slice)`, all records have non-empty `id` |
| `TestSearch_ExactMatch` | `query = "Luke"` (people) | 200, ≥1 hit, all hits contain "luke" (case-insensitive) |
| `TestSearch_Substring` | `query = "sky"` | 200, ≥1 hit, all hits contain "sky" |
| `TestSearch_CaseInsensitive` | `query = "LUKE"` | 200, same hits as `"luke"` |
| `TestSearch_NoMatch` | `query = "zzzz-no-match"` | 200, empty result, `count = 0` |
| `TestSearch_EmptyQuery` | `query = ""` | 200, same payload as `List` (D12) |

Plus a single `TestIntegration_EndToEnd` in `service/integration_test.go` that mounts the six handlers on an `httptest.NewServer` and replicates the example-client's call list: one `Get` per resource + a `ListPeople` + a `SearchPeople("")` + a `GetPerson("does-not-exist")` NotFound path.

### buf.gen.yaml (revised per D18 + D25)

```yaml
version: v2
managed:
  enabled: true
  override:
    - file_option: go_package_prefix
      module: github.com/example/swapi-grpc
      path: gen
clean: true
plugins:
  - local: protoc-gen-go
    out: .
    opt: paths=source_relative
  - local: protoc-gen-connect-go
    out: .
    opt: paths=source_relative
inputs:
  - directory: proto
```

> Note (D25): the `module:` line is a literal string in the YAML file. Make variables do not expand inside YAML, so `make rename-module NEW=...` rewrites this literal in the YAML (in addition to `go.mod` and the Makefile variable) in one shot.

### buf.yaml (revised per D23)

```yaml
version: v2
lint:
  use:
    - STANDARD
breaking:
  use:
    - FILE
```

(No `name:` field; the project is local-only. To publish to BSR, add `name: buf.build/<org>/swapi-grpc`.)

### Verification (end-to-end)

```bash
# 1. Lint
buf lint

# 2. Codegen is reproducible
rm -rf gen && make generate && git status   # gen/ appears, no other changes

# 3. Build everything
make build                                  # → ./bin/server

# 4. Tests
go test ./...                               # all green, ~60+ test cases

# 5. Server boots with default addr
./bin/server &
SERVER_PID=$!
# or: ./bin/server -addr :9000
# or: PORT=9001 ./bin/server

# 6. Reflection sees all 6 services + health
grpcurl -plaintext localhost:8080 list
# → grpc.health.v1.Health, grpc.reflection.v1alpha.ServerReflection,
#   swapi.v1.PeopleService, swapi.v1.FilmsService, swapi.v1.PlanetsService,
#   swapi.v1.SpeciesService, swapi.v1.StarshipsService, swapi.v1.VehiclesService

# 7. Health check
grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"service": ""}' localhost:8080 grpc.health.v1.Health/Check
# → {"status": "SERVING"}

# 8. Concrete RPC works
grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"id": "1"}' localhost:8080 swapi.v1.PeopleService/GetPerson
# → a Person message with name="Luke Skywalker"

# 9. List RPC works
grpcurl -plaintext localhost:8080 swapi.v1.PeopleService/ListPeople | head -50
# → a ListPeopleResponse with 82 people

# 10. Search RPC works
grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"query": "skywalker"}' localhost:8080 swapi.v1.PeopleService/SearchPeople
# → a ListPeopleResponse with Luke + Anakin + Sheev + etc.

# 11. Search with empty query returns full list
grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"query": ""}' localhost:8080 swapi.v1.PeopleService/SearchPeople | jq '.count'
# → 82

# 12. NotFound surfaces as CodeNotFound
grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"id": "this-does-not-exist"}' localhost:8080 swapi.v1.PeopleService/GetPerson
# → ERROR: ... code = NotFound ...

# 13. Malformed id surfaces as CodeInvalidArgument
grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"id": "###"}' localhost:8080 swapi.v1.PeopleService/GetPerson
# → ERROR: ... code = InvalidArgument ...

# 14. Connect protocol works (curl, no grpcurl needed)
curl -sS http://localhost:8080/swapi.v1.PeopleService/ListPeople \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' | jq '.count'
# → 82

# 15. CORS preflight works
curl -sS -i -X OPTIONS http://localhost:8080/swapi.v1.PeopleService/ListPeople \
  -H 'Origin: https://example.com' \
  -H 'Access-Control-Request-Method: POST' \
  -H 'Access-Control-Request-Headers: Content-Type'
# → Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

# 16. Example client works
go run ./cmd/client

# 17. Graceful shutdown via SIGTERM
kill -TERM $SERVER_PID
wait $SERVER_PID
echo "exit code: $?"   # → 0

# 18. Graceful shutdown via SIGINT
./bin/server &
SERVER_PID=$!
kill -INT $SERVER_PID
wait $SERVER_PID
echo "exit code: $?"   # → 0
```

### Reference (proto skeleton — PeopleService; the other 5 mirror this)

```proto
syntax = "proto3";
package swapi.v1;

// PeopleService exposes Star Wars characters. Numeric values such as
// `height` and `mass` are kept as `string` because swapi encodes
// "unknown" / "n/a" in those positions. Timestamps are ISO-8601 strings.
// FK fields are `repeated string` of IDs (not full URLs) — dereference
// them via the corresponding service (e.g. `homeworld_id` → PlanetsService.GetPlanet).
service PeopleService {
  rpc GetPerson(GetPersonRequest) returns (Person);
  rpc ListPeople(ListPeopleRequest) returns (ListPeopleResponse);
  // SearchPeople returns every person whose name contains `query`
  // (case-insensitive substring). An empty `query` returns the same
  // payload as ListPeople. Search never returns NotFound; an empty
  // result is a 200 with `count = 0`.
  rpc SearchPeople(SearchPeopleRequest) returns (ListPeopleResponse);
}

message Person {
  string id = 1;
  string name = 2;
  string height = 3;
  string mass = 4;
  string hair_color = 5;
  string skin_color = 6;
  string eye_color = 7;
  string birth_year = 8;
  string gender = 9;
  string homeworld_id = 10;        // singular FK (kept as string, not repeated)
  repeated string film_ids = 11;
  repeated string species_ids = 12;
  repeated string vehicle_ids = 13;
  repeated string starship_ids = 14;
  string created = 15;             // ISO 8601
  string edited = 16;
}

message GetPersonRequest  { string id = 1; }
message ListPeopleRequest {}
message ListPeopleResponse {
  repeated Person people = 1;
  int32 count = 2;
}
message SearchPeopleRequest { string query = 1; }
```

### Reference (directory layout — revised per D18, per-resource)

### Open questions / deferred items

- **OQ1** Real module path. Default `github.com/example/swapi-grpc` (D13). Trivial to change via `make rename-module`.
- **OQ2** swapi.info data shape (A1). Verified at S1 by the schema sanity check (D17 wires it as a Makefile prerequisite of `make generate`).
- **OQ3** Search scope. Currently `name` (or `title` for films). Multi-field search (e.g. on `manufacturer` for starships) deferred.
- **OQ4** Multi-language client gen. Connect-go's generators cover TS, Swift, Kotlin, etc.; out of scope for this initial cut.
- **OQ5** Web UI / playground. Defer.
- **OQ6** A "raw passthrough" RPC that returns the original swapi JSON. Defer.
- **OQ7** CORS tightening. Wildcard by default; lock-down via `connect.WithAllowedOrigins(...)` is a one-line change in `cmd/server/main.go` for production deployments.
- **OQ8** swapi.info id format confirmation. The plan supports both numeric and slug ids because A3 uses `string` everywhere. The first end-to-end run (S6 verification) will confirm which is actually in use; the result goes into the README under "Data notes."

data.candidatePlan:
## Candidate Plan — `swapi-grpc` (gRPC port of swapi.info)

> Revision 4 — addresses all blockers and warnings from revisions 1-3; the SOURCE path nitpick is addressed via D26 + the S1/A9 wording updates below.

### Objective

Create a fresh, opinionated Go project at `/Users/ycm/Developer/oss/swapi` that re-exposes the full swapi.info dataset (people, films, planets, species, starships, vehicles) as a **gRPC + Connect-protocol** service built on `connect-go` and `buf`. The server ships as a single self-contained binary; the data is embedded into the binary via `//go:embed`; the project compiles, passes `buf lint`, ships with a health check + reflection + CORS + graceful shutdown, and can be smoke-tested with `grpcurl`, `curl`, and a runnable example client.

### Constraints

- **Language/toolchain:** Go ≥ 1.22 (with `go 1.22` directive in `go.mod`), Buf v2 CLI, `protoc-gen-go`, `protoc-gen-connect-go` (v1.18+), `connectrpc/grpcreflect-go`, `connectrpc/connect-grpchealth-go`.
- **Transport:** gRPC over HTTP/2 and the Connect protocol over HTTP/1.1 from a single `net/http.Server`.
- **Data:** Embedded into the binary via `//go:embed` from a directory adjacent to the embedding Go file. No runtime network dependency.
- **API style:** Resource-oriented (one service per resource), unary RPCs only, no streaming.
- **Style:** snake_case in proto; stable field tags; proto package `swapi.v1`; `go_package` derived from a single `MODULE` Makefile variable, applied via `buf.gen.yaml`'s `go_package_prefix` (no sed).
- **License:**
  - Go code: **MIT** (`LICENSE`).
  - Embedded swapi data: derived from Wookieepedia / swapi; shipped under **CC-BY-SA 4.0** with a `NOTICE` file crediting Wookieepedia (https://starwars.fandom.com) and swapi (https://swapi.info).
  - README has an "Attribution & Data License" section.

### Non-goals

- Authentication, rate limiting, or any access control.
- Caching layer (data is small, embedded).
- Streaming RPCs, bidirectional flows, or server-push.
- Persisting edits — the dataset is read-only.
- A web UI, GraphQL gateway, or REST shim (use the Connect protocol directly).
- Auto-generating TS/Python/other-language clients (a Go example client is the demo).
- Cross-resource join RPCs.

### Module path

- **Default:** `github.com/example/swapi-grpc` (placeholder).
- **Single source of truth:** the Makefile exposes `MODULE ?= github.com/example/swapi-grpc`. `go.mod` and `buf.gen.yaml` (the literal `module:` line) both reference this string. The Makefile targets also reference the `MODULE` variable for `go mod edit -module` and similar; the YAML file holds a literal because Make variables do not expand inside YAML.
- **Override:** `make rename-module NEW=github.com/<you>/swapi-grpc` rewrites `go.mod`, the literal in `buf.gen.yaml`, and the Makefile variable in one shot. No proto file edits required.

### Data location

- The `//go:embed` directive cannot reach `data/*.json` at the repo root when the embedding Go file is in `internal/swapi/data/`.
- **Decision:** the canonical data location is `internal/swapi/data/data/`, adjacent to the embedding Go file. `make data` writes to `internal/swapi/data/data/` directly.
- `internal/swapi/data/data/SOURCE` records the upstream commit SHA used by the last `make data` run (informational; see A9). The SOURCE file lives alongside the JSON files in `internal/swapi/data/data/` so it ships with the embed.

### Assumptions

| # | Assumption | Resolution path |
|---|---|---|
| A1 | swapi.info data shape matches swapi.dev (field names, types, FK shape). | First step of S1 fetches all six lists and runs a programmatic schema sanity check (required fields present, expected counts ≥ 1, no duplicate IDs). If the check fails, `make data` exits non-zero and the plan revisits the `.proto` design before S2. |
| A2 | List responses are bare arrays; pagination is not used. | Confirmed by user choice. The proto still wraps `repeated X` in a `ListXResponse` message (gRPC requires this); no `next` / `previous` / `page` fields. |
| A3 | Resource IDs are preserved as **strings** (numeric `1` or slug `luke_skywalker` both fit). | Proto field is `string id`; loader does not coerce. **The example "luke_skywalker" is illustrative** — if swapi.info is confirmed to use numeric IDs at S1, the proto and tests still work because `string` is a strict superset. |
| A4 | FK fields are represented as `repeated string` of IDs (not full URLs). **Singular URL fields stay singular as `string`; repeated URL fields become `repeated string` of IDs.** The data loader strips the swapi base URL from each FK field and takes the trailing path segment as the ID. | Enforced centrally by `extractID(rawURL string) string` and `extractIDs(rawURLs []string) []string` helpers in `internal/swapi/data/data.go`. |
| A5 | Search is case-insensitive substring on `name` (or `title` for films). Empty `query` returns the same payload as `List` (D12). | Implemented server-side. Documented in the search RPC godoc and asserted by a unit test. |
| A6 | The `?schema` endpoint does not exist in swapi.info. | Not modeled. |
| A7 | Numeric-but-string fields (`height`, `mass`, `population`, `cost_in_credits`, etc.) stay as proto `string` because they can be `"unknown"` / `"n/a"`. | Documented in the proto file as a comment. |
| A8 | Timestamps (`created`, `edited`) stay as proto `string` (ISO-8601). | Documented. |
| A9 | Data snapshot is pinned to a specific upstream commit of `Juriy/swapi` (recorded in `internal/swapi/data/data/SOURCE`, alongside the JSON files). **Reproducibility is informational, not enforced** — the sanity test asserts shape (row counts, required fields, unique IDs) and fails `make data` if violated; the SHA in `SOURCE` is the audit trail. | `make data` always writes `SOURCE`; the shape check is what the test enforces. |
| A10 | The `internal/swapi/data/data/*.json` files are checked in; fetching them is a manual `make data` step, not part of the normal build. | Documented in the Makefile. |

### Resolved decisions

| # | Decision | Source |
|---|---|---|
| D1 | 6 services, 18 RPCs: `PeopleService` / `FilmsService` / `PlanetsService` / `SpeciesService` / `StarshipsService` / `VehiclesService`, each with `Get` + `List` + `Search`. | Grilling Q1 |
| D2 | Embedded data via `//go:embed data/*.json` from `internal/swapi/data/data.go` (data sits in `internal/swapi/data/data/`). | Grilling Q2 + revision 3 fix |
| D3 | No pagination — `List` returns the full embedded list in one response. | Grilling Q3 |
| D4 | gRPC server reflection via `connectrpc/grpcreflect-go` (`reflectv1.NewReflector` + `reflectv1.NewReflectServiceHandler`). | Grilling + reviewer |
| D5 | Browser CORS via `connect.WithCORS()` with **wildcard** origin (documented as demo-only in README). | Grilling + reviewer |
| D6 | Example client at `cmd/client/main.go`, including a NotFound call that prints the `connect.Code` and one Get per other resource. | Grilling + reviewer |
| D7 | Unit tests for each handler — one test file per resource under `internal/swapi/service/`. | Grilling + reviewer |
| D8 | gRPC Health v1 service via `connectrpc/connect-grpchealth-go`. Always-serving. | Reviewer |
| D9 | Configurable listen address via `-addr` flag with `PORT` env fallback (default `:8080`). | Reviewer |
| D10 | Graceful shutdown on `SIGINT` / `SIGTERM` with a 5s grace via `srv.Shutdown(ctx)`. End-to-end verification exercises **both** signals. | Reviewer |
| D11 | Error code contract — every handler returns `connect.NewError` with one of: `connect.CodeNotFound` (unknown id), `connect.CodeInvalidArgument` (malformed id; e.g. id containing characters not valid in the source's id charset), `connect.CodeInternal` (loader failure). | Reviewer |
| D12 | Empty search query returns the same payload as `List` (no error). | Grilling follow-up |
| D13 | Module path is a single Makefile variable with a `rename-module` target for post-clone rebranding. | Reviewer |
| D14 | Data license = CC-BY-SA 4.0 with `NOTICE` + README attribution section. Code license = MIT. | Grilling follow-up |
| D15 | The data loader uses **intermediate structs** that mirror the JSON field names, then transforms them into the generated proto types via a single conversion pass. **Direct `json.Unmarshal` into generated proto types is not used** because of the field renames (`homeworld` → `homeworld_id`, `films` → `film_ids`, `MGLT` → `mglt`, etc.). | Revision 3 fix |
| D16 | The `//go:embed` source directory is `internal/swapi/data/data/`, adjacent to the embedding Go file. | Revision 3 fix |
| D17 | The data sanity test is wired as a Makefile prerequisite of `make generate`, so a stale or broken data snapshot halts the build before codegen. | Revision 3 fix |
| D18 | Proto `go_package` is not set per-file. `buf.gen.yaml` uses `go_package_prefix` with a literal `module: github.com/example/swapi-grpc` and `path: gen`. The protoc-gen-go plugin generates Go code at `gen/swapi/v1/<fileBaseName>/<fileBaseName>.pb.go` (one Go package per `.proto` file). The protoc-gen-connect-go plugin generates `gen/swapi/v1/<fileBaseName>v1connect/<fileBaseName>.connect.go` and exposes the handler/client interfaces in the `<fileBaseName>v1connect` Go package. **Per-resource (per-file) naming throughout.** | Revision 3 + revision 4 fix |
| D19 | The test matrix exercises **malformed-id** cases (D11) with `id = "###"` or similar to assert `connect.CodeInvalidArgument`. | Revision 3 fix |
| D20 | Integration test (`internal/swapi/service/integration_test.go`) is written alongside the per-resource tests in S5; it does **not** import `cmd/client` (a main package). It replicates the example-client's call list against an `httptest.NewServer` to keep the test in-package. | Revision 3 fix |
| D21 | Handlers in S5 read fields directly: `person.Name`, `film.Title`, `species.Name`, etc. **No `Name()` method calls** (these are struct fields, not methods). | Revision 3 fix |
| D22 | `go.mod` ships with an explicit `go 1.22` directive. | Revision 3 fix |
| D23 | `buf.yaml` has no `name:` field by default; the project is local-only and is not published to BSR. (If the user later wants BSR, they can add `name: buf.build/<org>/swapi-grpc` to `buf.yaml`.) | Revision 3 fix |
| D24 | The Makefile exposes a `test-data` target (separate from the full `test` target) so `make generate` can depend on `make test-data` and not on the full unit test suite. | Revision 4 fix |
| D25 | The rendered `buf.gen.yaml` block contains the literal module string `github.com/example/swapi-grpc`. Make variables do not expand inside YAML, so `make rename-module` rewrites the literal in the YAML in addition to `go.mod` and the Makefile variable. | Revision 4 fix |
| D26 | The `SOURCE` file lives at `internal/swapi/data/data/SOURCE` (alongside the JSON files), not at `internal/swapi/data/SOURCE`. This keeps the SHA audit trail inside the embed-bundled directory. | Revision 4 fix |

### Implementation slices

| # | Slice | Deliverable | Verification |
|---|---|---|---|
| S0 | Bootstrap repo | `go.mod` (with `go 1.22` directive and all required deps), `Makefile` (with `MODULE` var; targets `data` `generate` `test-data` `test` `build` `run` `lint` `help` `rename-module`; `make generate` depends on `make test-data` per D24), `.gitignore` (`/gen`, `/bin`), `LICENSE` (MIT), `NOTICE` (Wookieepedia + swapi attribution, CC-BY-SA 4.0 pointer), `README.md` skeleton, `buf.yaml` (no `name:`), `buf.gen.yaml` (with the corrected `go_package_prefix` override per D18/D25) | `go mod tidy` works; `buf --version` works; `make help` lists every target (including `test-data`); `MODULE` variable substitution works; `make rename-module NEW=foo` is a no-op on the default module |
| S1 | Fetch & check in data | `make data` target does: (a) `git ls-remote` to capture the upstream commit of `Juriy/swapi`, (b) `curl` the six `data/<resource>.json` files from `raw.githubusercontent.com/Juriy/swapi/<sha>/data/...` to `internal/swapi/data/data/`, (c) write the SHA to `internal/swapi/data/data/SOURCE` (per D26), (d) `go test ./internal/swapi/data/...` (the `test-data` target) asserts required fields, ≥1 row per resource, unique IDs, and a sample-row FK transform. | `internal/swapi/data/data/*.json` non-empty; `internal/swapi/data/data/SOURCE` contains the SHA; `go test ./internal/swapi/data/...` passes; the sanity test fails `make data` when the loader is pointed at empty/wrong files; **`make generate` (a dependency of S2) is blocked by a failing sanity test** |
| S2 | Write protos | `proto/swapi/v1/{people,films,planets,species,starships,vehicles}.proto` — six files, one per resource. Each declares a `*Service` with `Get*` / `List*` / `Search*`, the resource message, request/response messages. **No `option go_package` per file** — D18. | `buf lint` clean |
| S3 | Generate code | `make generate` → `buf generate` produces **per-resource** Go packages: `gen/swapi/v1/people/people.pb.go`, `gen/swapi/v1/films/films.pb.go`, …, `gen/swapi/v1/planets/planets.pb.go`, `gen/swapi/v1/species/species.pb.go`, `gen/swapi/v1/starships/starships.pb.go`, `gen/swapi/v1/vehicles/vehicles.pb.go`. The connect-go plugin produces `gen/swapi/v1/people/peoplev1connect/people.connect.go` (and analogously for the other five resources), exposing `peoplev1connect.PeopleServiceHandler`, `peoplev1connect.NewPeopleServiceHandler`, `peoplev1connect.NewPeopleServiceClient`, etc. | `go build ./gen/...` succeeds; re-running `buf generate` after `rm -rf gen` produces an identical tree |
| S4 | Data loader | `internal/swapi/data/data.go` — single file with `//go:embed data/*.json`. **Two-pass load (D15):** (1) `json.Unmarshal` into an intermediate struct `rawPerson`, `rawFilm`, etc. that mirrors the JSON field names (e.g. `Homeworld string`, `Films []string`, `MGLT string`); (2) a `convert()` method transforms each intermediate struct into the generated proto type, applying `extractID` to FK URLs and renaming `MGLT` → `mglt`, `homeworld` → `homeworld_id`, `films` → `film_ids`, etc. The two helpers `extractID` and `extractIDs` are also tested directly. | `go test ./internal/swapi/data/...` covers: row counts, intermediate→proto rename correctness, `extractID("https://swapi.info/api/planets/1")` → `"1"`, `extractIDs(...)` preserves order and drops empty entries, required-field presence, ID uniqueness, and a sample full-record transform for one row per resource. |
| S5 | Service handlers + handler tests | `internal/swapi/service/{people,films,planets,species,starships,vehicles}.go` plus matching `<resource>_test.go` **and** `integration_test.go`. Each handler imports its own generated connect package (per S3's per-resource layout): `peoplev1connect`, `filmsv1connect`, etc. Each implements the `<resource>v1connect.<Resource>ServiceHandler` interface, holds a `*data.Data` field, and dispatches: `Get` → linear scan with case-insensitive id match, returning `connect.NewError(connect.CodeNotFound, ...)` on miss, `connect.NewError(connect.CodeInvalidArgument, ...)` on a clearly malformed id (e.g. id containing characters invalid for the source schema, see D11); `List` → return the slice; `Search` → case-insensitive substring on `.Name` / `.Title` (D21) with empty query returning the full list (per D12). | `go build ./internal/...` succeeds; `go test ./internal/swapi/service/...` passes the per-resource test matrix (see "Test cases" below) including the malformed-id cases (D19); the integration test in `integration_test.go` spins up `httptest.NewServer`, mounts the six handlers, and calls one Get per resource (replicating the example-client's call list) |
| S6 | Server entrypoint | `cmd/server/main.go` wires: all 6 service handlers via the per-resource generated `<resource>v1connect.New<Resource>ServiceHandler` factories (e.g. `peoplev1connect.NewPeopleServiceHandler`); gRPC reflection via `connectrpc.com/grpcreflect`; gRPC Health v1 via `connectrpc.com/connect-grpchealth-go`; `connect.WithCompressMinBytes(1024)` + `connect.WithCompressGzip()`; wildcard CORS via `connect.WithCORS()`; `http.Server` bound to `*addr` (flag `-addr`, env `PORT`); graceful shutdown trapping `SIGINT`/`SIGTERM` with a 5s `srv.Shutdown(ctx)` grace; structured logging via `log/slog`. | `./bin/server` starts on `:8080`; `./bin/server -addr :9000` starts on `:9000`; `PORT=9001 ./bin/server` starts on `:9001`; `kill -TERM $PID` exits 0 within 5s; `kill -INT $PID` exits 0 within 5s |
| S7 | Example client | `cmd/client/main.go` calls: `GetPerson("1")`, `ListPeople`, `SearchPeople("luke")`, `SearchPeople("")` (empty-query behavior), `GetPerson("does-not-exist")` (prints `connect.CodeNotFound`), plus one Get per other resource (`GetFilm("1")`, `GetPlanet("1")`, `GetSpecies("1")`, `GetStarship("2")`, `GetVehicle("4")`). Uses the per-resource `<resource>v1connect.New<Resource>ServiceClient(http.DefaultClient, baseURL)` factories. `baseURL` is a `-url` flag (default `http://localhost:8080`). | `go run ./cmd/client -url http://localhost:8080` prints expected output (Luke, full list, search hits, empty-query result identical to list, and a `NotFound` line) |
| S8 | _(deprecated, merged into S5)_ | _Per-resource test expansion and integration coverage now live in S5. The slice is kept here to preserve ordering for downstream readers but is a no-op; CI is gated on `go test ./...` from S5 onwards._ | n/a |
| S9 | README & polish | README with project description, data attribution (`Wookieepedia` + `swapi.info` + `CC-BY-SA 4.0`), `-addr` flag and `PORT` env docs, build/test/run commands, gRPC + Connect + curl examples, `make rename-module` usage, `make data` snapshot workflow. | Manual code-block review; all examples in the README also appear in the end-to-end verification block below |

### Per-resource proto field table

The convention is now explicit: **singular URL FKs stay as `string`; repeated URL FKs become `repeated string` of IDs.** This matches the species row in revision 2 and the reference proto's `string homeworld_id = 10;` on Person.

| Resource | Service | Singular fields (string) | `repeated string` FK IDs | int32 fields | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| people | `PeopleService` | `id`, `name`, `height`, `mass`, `hair_color`, `skin_color`, `eye_color`, `birth_year`, `gender`, `homeworld_id`, `created`, `edited` | `film_ids`, `species_ids`, `vehicle_ids`, `starship_ids` | none | `homeworld` is a singular URL → `string homeworld_id` (one element, kept singular per A4) |
| films | `FilmsService` | `id`, `title`, `opening_crawl`, `director`, `producer`, `release_date`, `created`, `edited` | `character_ids`, `planet_ids`, `starship_ids`, `vehicle_ids`, `species_ids` | `episode_id` (1–6) | `episode_id` is the only true int |
| planets | `PlanetsService` | `id`, `name`, `rotation_period`, `orbital_period`, `diameter`, `climate`, `gravity`, `terrain`, `surface_water`, `population`, `created`, `edited` | `resident_ids`, `film_ids` | none | |
| species | `SpeciesService` | `id`, `name`, `classification`, `designation`, `average_height`, `skin_colors`, `hair_colors`, `eye_colors`, `average_lifespan`, `homeworld_id`, `language`, `created`, `edited` | `people_ids`, `film_ids` | none | `homeworld` → `string homeworld_id` (singular) |
| starships | `StarshipsService` | `id`, `name`, `model`, `manufacturer`, `cost_in_credits`, `length`, `max_atmosphering_speed`, `crew`, `passengers`, `cargo_capacity`, `consumables`, `hyperdrive_rating`, `mglt`, `starship_class`, `created`, `edited` | `pilot_ids`, `film_ids` | none | JSON `MGLT` → proto `mglt` (snake_case) |
| vehicles | `VehiclesService` | `id`, `name`, `model`, `manufacturer`, `cost_in_credits`, `length`, `max_atmosphering_speed`, `crew`, `passengers`, `cargo_capacity`, `consumables`, `vehicle_class`, `created`, `edited` | `pilot_ids`, `film_ids` | none | |

### Per-resource test case matrix (S5)

For each of the 6 resources, the matching `<resource>_test.go` covers:

| Test | Input | Expected |
|---|---|---|
| `TestGet_Existing` | `id = "<known id>"` (the first row's id) | 200, message matches the embedded record (field-by-field equality) |
| `TestGet_NotFound` | `id = "this-does-not-exist"` | `connect.CodeNotFound` error, no payload |
| `TestGet_MalformedId` | `id = "###"` (clearly not a valid id in the source schema) | `connect.CodeInvalidArgument` error (D11, D19) |
| `TestList` | empty request | 200, `len(.<resource>s) == count`, count matches `len(embedded slice)`, all records have non-empty `id` |
| `TestSearch_ExactMatch` | `query = "Luke"` (people) | 200, ≥1 hit, all hits contain "luke" (case-insensitive) |
| `TestSearch_Substring` | `query = "sky"` | 200, ≥1 hit, all hits contain "sky" |
| `TestSearch_CaseInsensitive` | `query = "LUKE"` | 200, same hits as `"luke"` |
| `TestSearch_NoMatch` | `query = "zzzz-no-match"` | 200, empty result, `count = 0` |
| `TestSearch_EmptyQuery` | `query = ""` | 200, same payload as `List` (D12) |

Plus a single `TestIntegration_EndToEnd` in `service/integration_test.go` that mounts the six handlers on an `httptest.NewServer` and replicates the example-client's call list: one `Get` per resource + a `ListPeople` + a `SearchPeople("")` + a `GetPerson("does-not-exist")` NotFound path.

### buf.gen.yaml (revised per D18 + D25)

```yaml
version: v2
managed:
  enabled: true
  override:
    - file_option: go_package_prefix
      module: github.com/example/swapi-grpc
      path: gen
clean: true
plugins:
  - local: protoc-gen-go
    out: .
    opt: paths=source_relative
  - local: protoc-gen-connect-go
    out: .
    opt: paths=source_relative
inputs:
  - directory: proto
```

> Note (D25): the `module:` line is a literal string in the YAML file. Make variables do not expand inside YAML, so `make rename-module NEW=...` rewrites this literal in the YAML (in addition to `go.mod` and the Makefile variable) in one shot.

### buf.yaml (revised per D23)

```yaml
version: v2
lint:
  use:
    - STANDARD
breaking:
  use:
    - FILE
```

(No `name:` field; the project is local-only. To publish to BSR, add `name: buf.build/<org>/swapi-grpc`.)

### Verification (end-to-end)

```bash
# 1. Lint
buf lint

# 2. Codegen is reproducible
rm -rf gen && make generate && git status   # gen/ appears, no other changes

# 3. Build everything
make build                                  # → ./bin/server

# 4. Tests
go test ./...                               # all green, ~60+ test cases

# 5. Server boots with default addr
./bin/server &
SERVER_PID=$!
# or: ./bin/server -addr :9000
# or: PORT=9001 ./bin/server

# 6. Reflection sees all 6 services + health
grpcurl -plaintext localhost:8080 list
# → grpc.health.v1.Health, grpc.reflection.v1alpha.ServerReflection,
#   swapi.v1.PeopleService, swapi.v1.FilmsService, swapi.v1.PlanetsService,
#   swapi.v1.SpeciesService, swapi.v1.StarshipsService, swapi.v1.VehiclesService

# 7. Health check
grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"service": ""}' localhost:8080 grpc.health.v1.Health/Check
# → {"status": "SERVING"}

# 8. Concrete RPC works
grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"id": "1"}' localhost:8080 swapi.v1.PeopleService/GetPerson
# → a Person message with name="Luke Skywalker"

# 9. List RPC works
grpcurl -plaintext localhost:8080 swapi.v1.PeopleService/ListPeople | head -50
# → a ListPeopleResponse with 82 people

# 10. Search RPC works
grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"query": "skywalker"}' localhost:8080 swapi.v1.PeopleService/SearchPeople
# → a ListPeopleResponse with Luke + Anakin + Sheev + etc.

# 11. Search with empty query returns full list
grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"query": ""}' localhost:8080 swapi.v1.PeopleService/SearchPeople | jq '.count'
# → 82

# 12. NotFound surfaces as CodeNotFound
grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"id": "this-does-not-exist"}' localhost:8080 swapi.v1.PeopleService/GetPerson
# → ERROR: ... code = NotFound ...

# 13. Malformed id surfaces as CodeInvalidArgument
grpcurl -plaintext -d '{"id": "###"}' localhost:8080 swapi.v1.PeopleService/GetPerson
# → ERROR: ... code = InvalidArgument ...

# 14. Connect protocol works (curl, no grpcurl needed)
curl -sS http://localhost:8080/swapi.v1.PeopleService/ListPeople \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' | jq '.count'
# → 82

# 15. CORS preflight works
curl -sS -i -X OPTIONS http://localhost:8080/swapi.v1.PeopleService/ListPeople \
  -H 'Origin: https://example.com' \
  -H 'Access-Control-Request-Method: POST' \
  -H 'Access-Control-Request-Headers: Content-Type'
# → Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *

# 16. Example client works
go run ./cmd/client

# 17. Graceful shutdown via SIGTERM
kill -TERM $SERVER_PID
wait $SERVER_PID
echo "exit code: $?"   # → 0

# 18. Graceful shutdown via SIGINT
./bin/server &
SERVER_PID=$!
kill -INT $SERVER_PID
wait $SERVER_PID
echo "exit code: $?"   # → 0
```

### Reference (proto skeleton — PeopleService; the other 5 mirror this)

```proto
syntax = "proto3";
package swapi.v1;

// PeopleService exposes Star Wars characters. Numeric values such as
// `height` and `mass` are kept as `string` because swapi encodes
// "unknown" / "n/a" in those positions. Timestamps are ISO-8601 strings.
// FK fields are `repeated string` of IDs (not full URLs) — dereference
// them via the corresponding service (e.g. `homeworld_id` → PlanetsService.GetPlanet).
service PeopleService {
  rpc GetPerson(GetPersonRequest) returns (Person);
  rpc ListPeople(ListPeopleRequest) returns (ListPeopleResponse);
  // SearchPeople returns every person whose name contains `query`
  // (case-insensitive substring). An empty `query` returns the same
  // payload as ListPeople. Search never returns NotFound; an empty
  // result is a 200 with `count = 0`.
  rpc SearchPeople(SearchPeopleRequest) returns (ListPeopleResponse);
}

message Person {
  string id = 1;
  string name = 2;
  string height = 3;
  string mass = 4;
  string hair_color = 5;
  string skin_color = 6;
  string eye_color = 7;
  string birth_year = 8;
  string gender = 9;
  string homeworld_id = 10;        // singular FK (kept as string, not repeated)
  repeated string film_ids = 11;
  repeated string species_ids = 12;
  repeated string vehicle_ids = 13;
  repeated string starship_ids = 14;
  string created = 15;             // ISO 8601
  string edited = 16;
}

message GetPersonRequest  { string id = 1; }
message ListPeopleRequest {}
message ListPeopleResponse {
  repeated Person people = 1;
  int32 count = 2;
}
message SearchPeopleRequest { string query = 1; }
```

### Reference (directory layout — revised per D18, per-resource)

```
swapi/                                  # cwd
├── README.md                           # includes Attribution & Data License section
├── LICENSE                             # MIT (code)
├── NOTICE                              # Wookieepedia + swapi attribution; CC-BY-SA 4.0 pointer
├── Makefile                            # MODULE variable; targets data, generate, test-data, test, build, run, lint, help, rename-module
├── .gitignore                          # /gen, /bin
├── buf.yaml                            # no `name:` (local-only)
├── buf.gen.yaml                        # go_package_prefix: literal module + path
├── go.mod                              # go 1.22 directive
├── go.sum
├── proto/swapi/v1/{people,films,planets,species,starships,vehicles}.proto
├── gen/swapi/v1/                       # gitignored; regenerated by buf generate
│   ├── people/
│   │   ├── people.pb.go
│   │   └── peoplev1connect/people.connect.go
│   ├── films/
│   │   ├── films.pb.go
│   │   └── filmsv1connect/films.connect.go
│   ├── planets/
│   │   ├── planets.pb.go
│   │   └── planetsv1connect/planets.connect.go
│   ├── species/
│   │   ├── species.pb.go
│   │   └── speciesv1connect/species.connect.go
│   ├── starships/
│   │   ├── starships.pb.go
│   │   └── starshipsv1connect/starships.connect.go
│   └── vehicles/
│       ├── vehicles.pb.go
│       └── vehiclesv1connect/vehicles.connect.go
├── internal/swapi/
│   ├── data/
│   │   ├── data.go                     // //go:embed data/*.json; two-pass load (intermediate struct → proto type)
│   │   ├── data_test.go                // count, FK transform, extractID, required fields, unique IDs
│   │   └── data/                       # the actual JSON, adjacent to the embedder
│   │       ├── SOURCE                  # upstream commit SHA (informational; D26)
│   │       ├── people.json
│   │       ├── films.json
│   │       ├── planets.json
│   │       ├── species.json
│   │       ├── starships.json
│   │       └── vehicles.json
│   └── service/
│       ├── people.go                   # imports gen/swapi/v1/people/peoplev1connect
│       ├── people_test.go
│       ├── films.go
│       ├── films_test.go
│       ├── planets.go
│       ├── planets_test.go
│       ├── species.go
│       ├── species_test.go
│       ├── starships.go
│       ├── starships_test.go
│       ├── vehicles.go
│       ├── vehicles_test.go
│       └── integration_test.go         # httptest.NewServer + replication of example-client call list
├── cmd/
│   ├── server/main.go                  # -addr flag, PORT env, reflection, health, CORS, gzip, graceful shutdown (SIGINT+SIGTERM)
│   └── client/main.go                  # -url flag, exercises every RPC + NotFound + empty-query + MalformedId
```

### Open questions / deferred items

- **OQ1** Real module path. Default `github.com/example/swapi-grpc` (D13). Trivial to change via `make rename-module`.
- **OQ2** swapi.info data shape (A1). Verified at S1 by the schema sanity check (D17 wires it as a Makefile prerequisite of `make generate`).
- **OQ3** Search scope. Currently `name` (or `title` for films). Multi-field search (e.g. on `manufacturer` for starships) deferred.
- **OQ4** Multi-language client gen. Connect-go's generators cover TS, Swift, Kotlin, etc.; out of scope for this initial cut.
- **OQ5** Web UI / playground. Defer.
- **OQ6** A "raw passthrough" RPC that returns the original swapi JSON. Defer.
- **OQ7** CORS tightening. Wildcard by default; lock-down via `connect.WithAllowedOrigins(...)` is a one-line change in `cmd/server/main.go` for production deployments.
- **OQ8** swapi.info id format confirmation. The plan supports both numeric and slug ids because A3 uses `string` everywhere. The first end-to-end run (S6 verification) will confirm which is actually in use; the result goes into the README under "Data notes."
